About: It is about a Catholic title of the Blessed Virgin Mary associated with a 15th-century Byzantine icon and a purported Marian apparition.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with a 15th-century Byzantine icon.
It is associated with a purported Marian apparition.
It is associated with the Church of San Matteo in Via Merulana.
It is associated with the Church of Saint Alphonsus of Liguori in Rome.
It is associated with the novena to Our Mother of Perpetual Help.
It is associated with Pope Pius IX.
It is associated with the Congregation of the Most Holy Redeemer.
It is associated with the Virgin of the Passion in Eastern Orthodoxy.
It is associated with the Order of Saint Augustine.
It is associated with Andreas Rizo of Candia.
CreatedIn: It was created in the 15th century.
HasA: It has a Catholic title of the Blessed Virgin Mary.
It has a 15th-century Byzantine icon.
It has a purported Marian apparition.
It has a pontifical decree of canonical coronation.
It has an official formalized title Nostra Mater de Perpetuo Succursu.
It has a feast day on 27 June.
It has reproductions displayed in residential homes, commercial establishments, and public transportation.
It has an external door tymphanum of the Augustinian Church of San Matteo in Via Merulana.
It has a namesake cloistered monastery Madonna del Soccorso in Corleone, Palermo, Sicily.
It has a pious tale of origin.
HeldIn: It was held in the Church of San Matteo in Via Merulana from 1499 to 1798.
It is today permanently enshrined in the Church of Saint Alphonsus of Liguori in Rome.
It was held in the Church of Saint Mary in Posterula.
It was held in the Church of Saint Eusebius.
It was held in a side altar in the Church of Santa Maria in Posterula.
It was held in the private possession of a Roman merchant and his family.
It was held at Sant'Alfonso di Liguori on the Esquiline Hill in Rome.
It was held at the Church of St. Alphonsus of Ligouri.
